title: epigynum lachnocarpum
intro: Epigynum lachnocarpum, also known as the Monkey Cup or Monkey Flower, is a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae. Traditionally, the plant has been used in medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been attributed to the presence of various compounds such as flavonoids and alkaloids. The medicinal properties of Epigynum lachnocarpum have been found to provide health benefits including the treatment of fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, as well as its use as a natural remedy for wounds and skin conditions. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves and flowers are typically used to make tea or infusions, which can be consumed to reap its medicinal benefits.
